We can cross-link the existing article in a way that, you know, either adds more relevant links from your website or increases the number of links that you have.
And we can generate FAQ sections.
Okay.
Okay.
Thank you.
Is that what you're talking about, Tara?
Or were you talking about, like, how do we take content artifacts or, like, produce a piece of content and repurpose it across more?
Yeah, mean, it was kind of both.

I understand the refresh side of things, but I'm also thinking if we have a piece of content, like a longer form piece of content that Nate was talking about, how can we get the most out of that individual piece, whether that's, you know, an FAQ or a newsletter or another kind of lead-in overview doc, something like that?
Because that's something I've been trying to shoehorn a GPT to do and with varying levels of success.
Yeah, I see, I see.
I mean, there's more layers, right?

Like we come with research, how can we have a point of view from that research and have 10 content posts for social that are like more long form content posts for social that we can, you know, actually bring value to our audience instead of just like posting stuff they don't care about, right?
Newsletter, blog, things like that, right?
Yeah, I guess I'm thinking more on the distribution side of things than I am the actual asset.

Yeah, what we can do there, some examples like we've taken a bunch of like videos from a client on their YouTube or a bunch of podcasts, and then we've repurposed those into articles on their website.

I'm very about social as I am about other things.

Okay.
Follow up on all of that.

There is no rich media content that you guys can produce, be it be a video, be it be a podcast, anything like that.
It's only the written.
Word?

Understood.
Follow-up to that is, you talk about infographics, so what kind of design capabilities do you have?
Is that only GPT?

Do you have, like, would you have, like, an in-house designer, or, like, could we provide our design guidelines, and could that feed your image generation to create an infographic that is aligned to our brand's standards and guidelines?
Yeah, definitely something that's possible.
We have some forward-deployed design engineers and designers that we deploy on different engagements.
I think, you know, we would flesh that out when we have, like...
Like a tactical conversation around the scope and the price and certainly a possibility.
Okay, thanks.
Yeah, that's helpful.
That was also kind of what I was thinking because we can do a lot of the upfront collaborative strategy work.
Then once it gets to the creation and stretching it into as much as we can, I think that's where this would be valuable.
Yeah, we have a great design team, but they're small.
And so we don't want to take up all of their bandwidth with what could be produced.
so having some help in the design front would be really nice.
For sure.
And for a lot of the content that we start with, whether it's the blog or the guides, I guess what we've seen as well is like to drive results with AI search and to drive traffic.
The design isn't imperative there, right, with the way that like these LLMs are crawling and consuming.

And so, you know, that's, it may not be as needed for a lot of the work we do up front, but it could be needed when we think about more of the custom work or, you know, creating like the vendor, the vendor database for RAMP, like we created everything that you see there.
And there's a lot more design involved in that use case.
So that's just like a little bit more context.
Yeah.
And then all, all of this is designed for organic, ungated.

Is that a possibility as well, like, that you provide both an ungated, kind of what you normally do, and then one that's more tailored to be demand oriented?
So you're talking about, like, the paid media asset, like the...

For sure.
Are you saying that we would create the landing page with the form fill or just the content behind the form fill?
I guess potentially both, but my initial would be the content that would be behind the form fill.
Yeah, content behind the form fill, definitely.
Other scenario, need to talk to Marcel.
Thanks, Austin.
Okay.
On this side, thank you.
No, it's good.
It's good.
I love all, like, the ideas, and I wish I could, like, give you clarity on, like, what we can and can't do right now.

Sweet.
Any other questions?
Yeah, happy to answer more.

Yeah, mean, two questions is, well, maybe one is more just giving you context on, like, how our marketing team is structured and, like, potentially what that means for what an engagement would look like.

So I think you've kind of picked up.

Like, your audience first, we have four audiences.

Yep.

And then the last one...
And then Corporate Brand covers all of those potentially.

Like very similar to Scale, right?
Scale had separate companies to handle it.

Like Mercore, they hire people in mass.
And so we have this whole separate audience, which is massively buried.
ahead.
If
Um, because it depends on what type of talent we're hiring for, but their interest is finding opportunities to make money using their skills.
Right.
And that's, uh, that's to support all of the, the business units that we have.
And so there's, um, so there's actually a growth motion there as well, which no one here really represented that business unit, except for myself.
Um, but there's, yeah, so there's four, like very distinct audiences that all have their own growth motions.
And, um, so just so you're aware, that's kind of how our business works.
Um, and then maybe taking that into what, uh, an engagement could look like.
Um, each of them has their own nuances, like, um, Turing Intelligence probably is like the biggest wide open opportunity for this as we verticalize the audience.
And it's like, there is, there is very clear path to organic growth, you know, albeit enterprise organic growth is a little different than transaction organic growth, but, um, but there's that path.
With the AI research.
Searchers, it's like, they're very technical.
It's a very small group of people, right?

We shouldn't start there.

Well, budget-wise, they're at the bottom.

Yeah.

But they've been highly neglected.

And we're going to end up just spending a bunch of money on paid, like trying to attract the talent.
But we used to have this major organic motion because we had content across every single skill that existed in our target audience, right?
Yeah.
And so we had multiple content categories.
So anything?

Anything that you see that's KB, like knowledge-based KB, or job descriptions, or interview questions, or resume content categories, we have these massive content categories that really haven't been touched since 2022.
So refresh could be like an easy win there, probably.
Yes, it could be, but the resources in order to partner with you on that are limited, right?
We don't have a marketing team doing that.
We have like a single person kind of handling that right now, like the growth on that side.
And it's mostly paid, right?
It's like post jobs for these roles, hire those people.
So yes, there is a big opportunity for growth.

But when it comes to like showing, hey, the ROI for the $20,000 a month that we're spending with you guys, there's not going to be a big appetite for the result of that to be like, a look at, look at this organic growth.
Oh, for the talent audience.
Does that make sense?
Makes perfect sense.
Yeah, I think I really appreciate that.
That helps me kind of navigate it.

And so if we were focused on TI and TI talent to start, you know, more on the enterprise side, who would be like the, and I know there's a big audience, so who would be like the core team that we would work with?
That would be Nate and me.
Yeah, people you got on here right now.
the ones that stay behind.
We're the ones interested in this.
Yeah, I'm doing a lot of the verticalized content, so you'd be working with me on a lot of that.
And then Nate is doing a lot of the industry demand side of things, so.
Okay, cool.
And how much content, like, let's say editorial, are you creating today, like on a weekly basis, would you say, on the site?
We're probably at almost once a week now, Nate, right?
I'd say one to two pieces a week.
We've really started to ramp up, so.
Honestly, more than that now, yeah.
Yeah, it's a minimum.
Yeah.

Minimum a piece a week that, I mean, we do like, it's called like an AI forward blog, and then we're also trying to capitalize on other thought leaderships, events that we attend, you know, things that our leaders are saying, things that, you know, prominent news, and trying to capitalize on, you know, buzzwords, AI bubble, like things like that.
So at least, and then with Tara's stuff, yeah, I'd say our goal is to probably do two or three a week.
Yeah, and that's pretty much where we're sitting at right now.
So as far as like actual production is going, we've got a decent engine, but as we start to scale with additional industries and all of that, we'll probably need some more help, so.

Yeah, and then there's my side, like I'm held accountable for SEO growth, right, and so there's a whole bunch of SEO, like SEO, GEO, AEO opportunities that like we can send to Tara and Nate, but like they're getting pressure from the business to have like the.
Business cases, which aren't necessarily always aligned one-to-one, right?
And so there's also bottom-up pressure and there's top-down pressure.

That's why I called out SEO, GEO content as something that we could use this for, because I know that Vasim is busting his behind trying to get us keywords and things to write, but, you know, we've got different priorities, and if we can create those things a little bit faster, that would be great.
And that's also why I asked about repurposing and distribution, because, I mean, we've got a decent engine going right now, but I think there's a lot more we can do with it.

Yeah, I think that, I think high level seems like there could be, like, a really awesome fit between, like, what your team works on, what our team works on, and really having our team, like, focus on what we're best at, which is the SEO, GEO side.
Yeah.
My like, he wants SEO growth.
My CMO doesn't think that it's possible to grow enterprise businesses with...
SEO, and then we don't have a good way to attribute it, right?
And so, like, if you can come in and say, look, we did this, it accelerated pipeline, right?
That would be massive.
Okay, awesome.

Yeah, I do feel like a lot of our customers, you know, they're enterprise B2B, they sell large contract values, you know, obviously we have, like, the augment codes and the lovables that are more, as many signups as possible.

Um, but not something we haven't seen before, and, uh, I think the best next step, tell me, I guess, what your ideal timeline looks like, and I'm not assuming you guys are moving forward with us, just wanting to match your pace in terms of when you want to have a partner or a technology in place.

Well, from a budget perspective, like, the beginning of the, like, getting through this process, and then having something set that's, like, January 1, I don't know that there's, uh, it's going to be more challenging to get budget approval for the last.
The two months of the year than it is for January 1.
So that I think is a potential timeline is like having something that starts first of the year.
But if it were up to me, it would be faster.
Yeah, I mean, we're honestly booked through the end of the year and we're really being intentional about like which January partnerships we're going to have.
So I think timeline is going to be the same on our side too.
What's the best next step?
I'm thinking that maybe like Weston, like, you know, you and I or the whole team here could do like a 30-minute focused follow-up after like I really dig into your doc.
I, you know, talk to Marcel about some of the use cases that you're interested in that are more custom.
And I can give you like a clearer look at like scope and what the future might look like.
And then I can also like really just understand the business better and ask you some more focused questions about like the kind of content we would create.
Does that seem like a good next step?
right.
Take Let's
Yes.
then, Tara, Nathan, I mean, keep me honest, on your side, like, Maya owns budget, I'm assuming, and would she need to be included in those discussions?
Yeah, probably from a budget standpoint.
So she's aware of, mean, we've had some conversations, but yeah, I think she would need to be in the room just to make sure that things are aligned, because, so, yeah, I would say yes.
let's actually do this, I think this makes more sense, like, y'all understand our pricing at a high level, and, like, where we would start.
We try to optimize for, you know, starting at 15, like, not much higher than that, maybe one work stream, unless you guys would prefer to do more.

Like, why don't we do our next conversation, is just really understanding the use case, and the kind of content that you'd want us to create, and then the conversation after that, that can be, like, the scoping and the pricing conversation, and I'll bring Marcel to that one as well.
Does that work?
That sounds fine to me.
Yeah.
I just realized, like, if we talk scope next step, like, we're just getting ahead of, like, understanding the use case we execute on first.
Yeah.
Awesome.
Awesome.
You guys want to bring that forward?
Like, hey, here's our use cases, prioritize what we want to, like, types of content we want to produce?
Yeah.
Yeah.
I'll share a bunch of resources.
And Tara or Nate, if you could maybe share, like, I don't know, three to five content examples of just what you think great looks like internally and maybe, like, some competitor content as well.
I can look through that before the next meeting and we can get really focused around, like, okay, like, here's the topics that we're looking at.
Here's the personas within those different enterprise, within those different business units.
And also even, like, here's the titles, right, that we go after.
Yeah.

So.
Hopefully that will go.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Evaluate GrowthxAI's content creation services for Turing's marketing needs.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=29.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Initial Focus on Editorial:** GrowthxAI's core service is organic editorial content. The paid media use case is likely out of scope, but Tyler will confirm with CEO Marcel.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=71.0)
  - [**Two-Phase Engagement:** A mandatory 8-week Strategy Sprint (est. $15k) defines the content engine. This is followed by Growth Execution, where additional workstreams (e.g., newsletters) can be added for $9-15k each.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
  - [**Turing's Initial Priority:** The Turing Intelligence (TI) and TI Talent business units, as they have the clearest organic growth path and budget. The Talent audience is a high-need area but lacks budget.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=2604.0)
  - [**Next Steps:** A follow-up meeting is scheduled to define specific content use cases, with a target start date of January 1, 2026, to align with Turing's budget cycle.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=3087.0)

## Topics

### Turing's Content Challenges & Needs

  - [**Current AI Use:** Manual, task-specific GPT use for revisions and derivative pages; no automated workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=445.0)
  - [**Strategic Needs:**](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=546.0)
      - [Competitive analysis & foundational research for content briefs.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=546.0)
      - [AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) strategy to capture AI search traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=591.0)
  - [**Repurposing & Distribution:** High interest in repurposing long-form content (e.g., research reports) into social posts, newsletters, and FAQs.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=2165.0)
  - [**Design Support:** Need for design help (e.g., infographics) to offload work from a small internal team.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=2365.0)
  - [**Gated Content:** Interest in creating gated assets for demand generation, separate from organic content.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=2485.0)

### GrowthxAI's Solution & Process

  - [**Core Offering:** An end-to-end partner providing an internal platform and expert team for scalable, quality content.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=166.0)
  - [**Process Overview:**](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
      - [**Phase 1: Strategy Sprint (8 weeks, est. $15k)**](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
          - [Defines ICP, brand voice, and custom AI workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
          - [Ingests Turing's resources to build a custom knowledge base.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
          - [Publishes 5-10 articles to establish the quality bar.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
          - [Turing can opt out after this phase.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
      - [**Phase 2: Growth Execution**](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=754.0)
          - [Scales to daily publishing based on the roadmap from Phase 1.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=754.0)
          - [Additional workstreams (e.g., newsletters) can be added for $9-15k each.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=775.0)
  - [**Content Creation Workflow:**](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=1478.0)
      - [**Brief Generation:** Agentic workflow analyzes SERP, competitors, and search intent.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=1497.0)
      - [**Deep Research:** Uses tools like EXA/TAP and a custom researcher; loops until a quality threshold is met.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=1594.0)
      - [**Drafting:** Generates content, adds cross-links, and validates against guidelines.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=1539.0)
      - [**Fact-Checking:** Verifies every passage against sources; suggests rewrites if confidence is low.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=1655.0)
      - [**Human Oversight:** Experts are in the loop for edits and to guide the AI with instructions.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=1677.0)

### Turing's Audience & Prioritization

  - [**Four Distinct Audiences:**](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=2604.0)
    1.  [**Turing Intelligence (TI):** Enterprises using deployed AI.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=2627.0)
    2.  [**TI Talent:** Scaling businesses using staff augmentation.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=2627.0)
    3.  [**TA2:** AI researchers and labs.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=2627.0)
    4.  [**Talent Audience:** Developers seeking work.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=2627.0)
  - [**Initial Priorities:**](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=2604.0)
      - [**TI & TI Talent:** Highest priority due to clear organic growth paths and budget. The core team for this is Tara and Nathan.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=2733.0)
      - [**TA2:** Lower priority; the audience is highly technical and small.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=2733.0)
      - [**Talent Audience:** High need (neglected content since 2022) but low budget appetite for organic growth ROI.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=2773.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Tyler:**](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=3149.0)
      - [Review the Turing use case document.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=3149.0)
      - [Confirm with Marcel the scope for paid media and gated content.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=128.0)
      - [Share GrowthxAI resources with the Turing team.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=3263.0)
  - [**Tara & Nathan:**](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=3253.0)
      - [Share 3–5 examples of "great" internal content and competitor content.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=3253.0)
      - [Refine and prioritize specific content use cases for the next meeting.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=3253.0)
  - [**All:**](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=3149.0)
      - [Schedule a 30-min follow-up to define content use cases.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=3149.0)
      - [Schedule a subsequent meeting with Marcel (GrowthxAI CEO) and Maya (Turing budget owner) to discuss scoping and pricing.](https://fathom.video/share/oairk3HdBGUZ2Mpyxzp3bXa5XJ3Xn77h?tab=summary&timestamp=3183.0)

Send 3–5 internal/competitor content examples to Tyler
Send 3–5 internal/competitor content examples to Tyler
Flesh out TI/TI Talent use cases for Tyler
